refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency performance metric and its impact on safe teleoperation, clarifying the measurable acceptance criteria.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication path under defined network conditions using telemetry logs and network monitoring tools.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission to prevent latency spikes.
FR3: Monitor and report latency metrics in real-time.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocols and data encoding to reduce latency.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to process commands closer to the vehicle.
DP3: Use dedicated network links with QoS prioritization for teleoperation traffic.
DP4: Real-time latency monitoring and adaptive control to mitigate latency spikes.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocols and data encoding to reduce latency.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol optimization and network link quality.
Dependency on vehicle hardware processing speed.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically decoupled from other system functions but requires coordination with network and vehicle modules for effective implementation.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unpredictable network behavior.
Assumes vehicle hardware can support optimized protocols without added delay.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP3 (dedicated network links) if network conditions degrade or latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication stack responsible for teleoperation command and telemetry data handling.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting teleoperation data transmission.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and task instructions received from NGHE Solution or Site Manager.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as DP1 maps directly to Teleoperation module; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle module participates in telemetry but does not own latency optimization logic.
NGHE Solution oversees system but does not implement latency optimization directly.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not control teleoperation latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency optimization logic is best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maintain clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module processing speed may cause latency outside Teleoperation control.
Network Link variability may impact latency despite Teleoperation optimization.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor cross-module latency dependencies and consider co-ownership or interface contracts with Vehicle and Network Link modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear latency reduction focus and measurable criteria.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP1 is well contained in Teleoperation module.
Cross-module dependencies on Vehicle and Network Link are acknowledged but manageable.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the primary owner of latency optimization.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry and network monitoring tools within Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency optimization to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Establish interface contracts with Vehicle and Network Link modules to manage latency dependencies.
Plan for fallback or mitigation strategies if network conditions degrade.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the acceptance criteria with clear ownership and verification paths. No further decomposition is needed at this stage.

revise.why: Current FR, DP, and IM are sufficiently defined and aligned.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Vehicle hardware supports optimized communication protocols.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network variability.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational network conditions.
Verification of telemetry data accuracy and timeliness.
